rows.label: TimSort
columns: Java default (Arrays.sort on objects). Hybrid Merge+Insertion.

rows.label: Quicksort
columns: Java default (Arrays.sort on primitives). Pivot selection matters.

rows.label: Mergesort
columns: Guaranteed O(n log n). Good for linked lists. Used in TimSort.

rows.label: Heapsort
columns: In-place, no worst-case degradation. Poor cache locality.

rows.label: Insertion Sort
columns: Best for small/nearly sorted arrays. Used in TimSort for small runs.

rows.label: Counting Sort
columns: k = value range. Linear time but only for small integer ranges.

rows.label: Radix Sort
columns: k = number of digits/characters. Linear for fixed-width integers.

rows.label: Bucket Sort
columns: Uniform distribution assumption. Worst case if all in one bucket.

rows.label: DFS
columns: Cycle detection, topological sort, connected components

rows.label: Dijkstra
columns: Shortest path in weighted graph; use PriorityQueue
